---
title: "Positive EV Sports Betting"
subtitle: "NFL bookmaker odds, calibration, and value strategy (2020–2025)"
format:
  pptx:
    reference-doc: "Positive EV Sportbetting.pptx"
    slide-level: 1
  html:
    theme: default
    css: styles.css
    toc: false
page-layout: article
---

# Why this report
- De-vig NFL odds (2020–2025) to estimate true win probabilities.
- Check calibration and bookmaker edge.
- Test a simple +EV betting rule.
- Data: The Odds API snapshots → flattened by bookmaker/market/outcome, deduped by game.
- True prob proxy: median no-vig prices from sharp books; calibration via beta + BBQ models (Brier/log-loss/ECE).
- Backtest: flat $100 stake on any +EV offer (stacked bets per game = aggressive upper bound).

# Agenda
- Data and tools used to turn odds into estimated truth.
- How we calibrate and compute EV (math + models).
- Backtest design and risk assumptions.
- Key results (home/away, calibration, bookmaker EV, +EV strategy).
- Practical takeaways and future work.

# Data coverage & sources
- Seasons: NFL regular seasons 2020–2025 (game JSONs in repo).
- Source: The Odds API snapshots (home/away moneyline markets).
- Books: sharp list to form priors; all available books scanned for EV.
- Cleaning: group by game date/home/away → drop duplicate book rows.
- Outcomes: historical results joined to snapshots for training/testing.

# Workflow at a glance
- Odds JSON → implied probs (decimal/american) → remove vig per market.
- Compute sharp-book median as `p_prior` (consensus true prob proxy).
- Build training set by joining priors to labeled results.
- Fit calibrators (beta, BBQ) and evaluate Brier, log-loss, ECE.
- Score snapshots with calibrated probs → compute EV per book/outcome.
- Scan for +EV bets and backtest cumulative profit and per-book ROI.

# From odds to true probabilities
- Implied probability (decimal odds): `p_implied = 1 / odds`.
- No-vig step on two-way markets: scale implied probs so they sum to 1.
- True-prob proxy: median no-vig `p` across sharp books (consensus).
- Calibrated probability = model-adjusted `p` to reduce bias at extremes.
- These calibrated `p` values feed EV calculations and backtests.

# Calibration models
- **Beta calibrator**: parametric, smooths probabilities toward observed frequencies.
- **BBQ (Bayesian binning into quantiles)**: nonparametric; averages over binnings to capture local bias.
- Metrics tracked: Brier, log-loss, expected calibration error, sample size.
- Goal: reduce under/overconfidence so EV math reflects realistic win rates.

# Expected value & line shopping
- EV per outcome: `(decimal_odds * p_win) - 1`.
- Positive EV if bettor’s estimated `p_win` beats the book’s implied chance.
- Line shopping matters: pick the book with highest EV for the same game.
- In backtest we allow multiple +EV books per game → aggressive but upper bound.

# Backtest setup
- Rule: whenever any book shows +EV, bet $100 on that side.
- Time span: 2020–2025 snapshots; both home and away sides considered.
- Exposure: multiple books can trigger per game (stacking risk).
- Metrics: win rate, total profit, ROI curve, per-book EV summary.
- Purpose: stress-test whether calibrated +EV signals pay off at scale.

# Figure 1 - Home vs Away
- Home win rate: 53.9% vs de-vig true prob 54.9%.
- Away win rate: 46.1% vs de-vig true prob 45.1%.
- Market (after vig removed) tracks reality closely.
- Games grouped by date/home/away to avoid sportsbook duplication.

![Home vs Away: Actual vs True Probability](fig1_home_vs_away%20(1).png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

# Figure 2 - Odds Calibration
- Mid-range (50%–70%) is well aligned with actual win rates.
- Underdogs: odds overestimate their chances (overpriced).
- Heavy favorites: odds slightly underestimate them (underpriced).
- Probability bins compare implied vs actual; dashed line = perfect calibration.
- Bias at extremes suggests value may appear on big favorites and against long-shot home teams.

![Calibration of Sportsbook Odds](fig2_odds_calibration.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

# Figure 3 - Bookmaker EV
- All books show negative average EV (house edge persists).
- Some books are less negative (closer to zero).
- Line shopping matters: pick the least-bad price per game.
- Average home/away EV combined per book; bars left of 0 are bad for bettors.

![Average EV by Bookmaker](fig3_bookmaker_ev.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

# Figure 4 - +EV Betting Strategy
- Rule: bet $100 whenever any book offers +EV on a game (may place multiple bets per game).
- 2,913 bets | 44.6% win rate | $21,216 profit.
- Profitable despite <50% win rate because wins happen at better odds.
- Stacking per book makes this an upper-bound, high-exposure result; one-bet-per-game would be lower.
- Cumulative curve shows volatile path but positive trend.

![Cumulative Profit for +EV Bets](fig4_ev_strategy_profit.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

# Limitations & next steps
- Strategy risk: stacked bets per game inflate exposure; cap to one book/game or size via Kelly/fixed limits.
- Market scope: only NFL sides; extend to totals/props/other sports and test live-odds latency/slippage.
- Modeling: explore richer priors, more calibration diagnostics, and sensitivity to hold assumptions.
- Data freshness: snapshots are point-in-time; real-time odds drift could erode observed edge.

# Takeaways
- De-vigged odds mirror outcomes; reliable starting point for EV.
- Probability extremes show bias: underdogs overpriced, heavy favorites underpriced.
- No book is +EV overall; line shopping trims the house edge.
- +EV strategy backtest is promising but aggressive; constrain exposure for realistic deployment.
